title: physalis viscosa var. elliottii
intro: Physalis viscosa var. elliottii, commonly known as the sticky ground cherry, is a perennial plant native to the southeastern United States and belongs to the nightshade family (Solanaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which help in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Physalis viscosa var. elliottii include reducing pain and inflammation, treating fever and cold, and supporting respiratory health. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
